nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Stoneham
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Kent): habitational name from North and South Stoneham (Hampshire), Stonham Aspal (Suffolk), Little Stonham (Suffolk), or Stoneham Farms in South Malling (Sussex). The Hampshire and Suffolk placenames derive from Old English stān ‘stone, rock’ + hām ‘village, homestead’, while the Sussex placename derives from Old English stān + hamm ‘water meadow’.

variants.name: Stonum
meanings: Probably an altered form of English   Stoneham  .

nameMeanings.name: John
meanings: Greek: the Lord gave graciously; whom Jehovah gave; whom Jehovah bestowed.

en.lifeSummary: Mrs. John de Stoneham was born in 1336, in Stonham Aspal, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom. She had at least 1 son with John de Stoneham. She died in her hometown.

ko.lifeSummary: Mrs. John de Stoneham(은)는 1336년 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 스토함 아스팔에서 태어났다. 그녀는 John de Stoneham(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 그녀의 고향에서 사망했다.
